title: Citizenship · 1800s & Recent History
description: The Louisiana Purchase, the Civil War and Lincoln, the World Wars, the Cold War, the civil rights movement, 9/11, and other important events.

cards.front: What territory did the United States buy from France in 1803?
cards.back: The Louisiana Territory (the Louisiana Purchase). It roughly doubled the size of the United States.
cards.quizAnswer: The Louisiana Territory (the Louisiana Purchase).

cards.front: Name one war fought by the United States in the 1800s.
cards.back: The War of 1812, the Mexican-American War, the Civil War, or the Spanish-American War. Any one is accepted.
cards.quizAnswer: The Civil War (also the War of 1812, Mexican-American, or Spanish-American War).

cards.front: Name the U.S. war between the North and the South.
cards.back: The Civil War (1861–1865). It was fought between the northern states (the Union) and the southern states (the Confederacy).
cards.quizAnswer: The Civil War.

cards.front: Name one problem that led to the Civil War.
cards.back: Slavery, economic reasons, or states' rights. Disagreement over slavery was the central cause.
cards.quizAnswer: Slavery, economic differences, and states' rights.
distractors: High taxes imposed by Great Britain.
The purchase of the Louisiana Territory.
Women being denied the right to vote.

cards.front: What was one important thing that Abraham Lincoln did?
cards.back: He freed the enslaved people (Emancipation Proclamation), saved (or preserved) the Union, and led the nation during the Civil War. Any one is accepted.
cards.quizAnswer: He freed the enslaved people and preserved the Union during the Civil War.
distractors: He wrote the Declaration of Independence.
He led the army in the Revolutionary War.
He bought the Louisiana Territory from France.
He was the first President of the U.S.
He served as a general in World War II.

cards.front: What did the Emancipation Proclamation do?
cards.back: It freed the enslaved people — specifically those in the Confederate states. Issued by Lincoln in 1863, it made ending slavery a goal of the Civil War.
cards.quizAnswer: Freed the enslaved people in the Confederate states.
distractors: Gave women the right to vote.
Gave Black men the right to vote.
Ended the Civil War between the states.
Granted citizenship to all former slaves.
Removed the poll tax from elections.

cards.front: What did Susan B. Anthony do?
cards.back: She fought for women's rights and for civil rights. She was a leader of the movement for women's right to vote.
cards.quizAnswer: She fought for women's rights and civil rights.
distractors: She led the civil rights march on Washington.
She freed the enslaved people in the South.
She was the first woman in Congress.
She served as the first female justice.

cards.front: Name one war fought by the United States in the 1900s.
cards.back: World War I, World War II, the Korean War, the Vietnam War, or the (Persian) Gulf War. Any one is accepted.
cards.quizAnswer: World War I, World War II, the Korean War, or the Vietnam War.

cards.front: Who was President during World War I?
cards.back: Woodrow Wilson. He led the United States during World War I (1914–1918; the U.S. entered in 1917).
cards.quizAnswer: Woodrow Wilson.

cards.front: Who was President during the Great Depression and World War II?
cards.back: Franklin Roosevelt (FDR). He led the nation through the Great Depression and most of World War II.
cards.quizAnswer: Franklin Roosevelt.

cards.front: Who did the United States fight in World War II?
cards.back: Japan, Germany, and Italy — the Axis Powers. The United States and its allies defeated them.
cards.quizAnswer: Japan, Germany, and Italy (the Axis Powers).

cards.front: Before he was President, Eisenhower was a general. What war was he in?
cards.back: World War II. Dwight D. Eisenhower was the Supreme Allied Commander in Europe before becoming President.
cards.quizAnswer: World War II.

cards.front: During the Cold War, what was the main concern of the United States?
cards.back: Communism. The United States worked to contain the spread of communism, led by the Soviet Union.
cards.quizAnswer: Communism.

cards.front: What movement tried to end racial discrimination?
cards.back: The civil rights movement. It worked to end segregation and secure equal rights for Black Americans, especially in the 1950s and 1960s.
cards.quizAnswer: The civil rights movement.

cards.front: What did Martin Luther King, Jr. do?
cards.back: He fought for civil rights and worked for equality for all Americans. He was a leader of the civil rights movement.
cards.quizAnswer: He fought for civil rights and worked for equality for all Americans.
distractors: He fought for women's right to vote.
He freed the enslaved people in the South.
He served as the first Black President.
He led the army in World War II.
He wrote the Declaration of Independence.

cards.front: What major event happened on September 11, 2001, in the United States?
cards.back: Terrorists attacked the United States. Hijacked planes struck the World Trade Center in New York and the Pentagon.
cards.quizAnswer: Terrorists attacked the United States.
distractors: The United States entered World War II.
The Cold War came to an end.
The civil rights march reached Washington.
The stock market crashed, starting a depression.
The Persian Gulf War began overseas.

cards.front: Name one American Indian tribe.
cards.back: Examples: Cherokee, Navajo, Sioux, Apache, Iroquois, Creek, Hopi, Choctaw, Pueblo, Seminole, Mohegan, Lakota, and many others.
cards.quizAnswer: Cherokee, Navajo, or Sioux (among many others).

cards.front: Why did the United States enter World War I?
cards.back: Because of German submarine attacks on U.S. ships, the Zimmermann Telegram, and to support its allies. The U.S. declared war on Germany in 1917.
cards.quizAnswer: German submarine attacks and the Zimmermann Telegram, to support its allies.
distractors: Because of the bombing of Pearl Harbor.
To stop the spread of communism abroad.
Because terrorists attacked New York City.
To free the enslaved people in Europe.
Because Britain taxed the colonies heavily.

cards.front: Why did the United States enter World War II?
cards.back: Because of the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941. The U.S. declared war the next day.
cards.quizAnswer: Because Japan attacked Pearl Harbor in 1941.
distractors: To stop the spread of communism abroad.
To support the colonists against Britain.

cards.front: Name one leader of the civil rights movement.
cards.back: Examples: Martin Luther King, Jr., Rosa Parks, John Lewis, Thurgood Marshall, Malcolm X, and others.
cards.quizAnswer: Martin Luther King, Jr., Rosa Parks, or John Lewis (among others).

cards.front: Who were the Presidents during the Korean War?
cards.back: Harry Truman (under whom the war began in 1950) and Dwight Eisenhower (under whom it ended in 1953). Either is accepted.
cards.quizAnswer: Harry Truman and Dwight Eisenhower.

cards.front: Why is the United Nations important?
cards.back: It gives countries a place to discuss and try to resolve world problems and to provide humanitarian aid. It was founded after World War II to promote peace.
cards.quizAnswer: It lets countries discuss and resolve world problems and provide aid.
distractors: It writes the laws of the United States.
It commands the United States military forces.
It elects the President of the United States.
It sets the tax rates for member nations.
It decides court cases between U.S. states.
